name: Mozilla Corp.
description: Firefox Networking (Necko) Team but working with Android Team as well 🦊

Extended DNS over HTTPS (DoH) in Firefox for Android (Kotlin, Jetpack Compose, Redux-based architecture), integrating GeckoView API (via Java) to deliver desktop-level privacy for over 178 million mobile users

Revamped the C++ networking error pipeline (neterror) and redesigned the JS-based error pages in Firefox, partnering with the content team to refine messaging and improve accessibility, reducing related tickets by 25%

name: Mozilla Corp.
description: Integrated Microsoft Entra Single Sign-On for Firefox on macOS via Apple’s Authorization API in Objective-C, potentially benefiting 7+ million users, and represented Mozilla in cross-company discussion with Microsoft

Enhanced the URL parsing layer (C++, JavaScript, Rust) for 25+ URL schemes and query encodings in Firefox, elevating Firefox Nightly’s Interop 2024 URL score to 99.3% and solidifying cross-platform compatibility
Enhanced spoofing detection for 70+ IDNs in C++, preventing malicious alpha-numeric lookalike combinations

Migrated 50+ networking Telemetry probes to the Glean API and built a real-time dashboard on Redash, used weekly by 10+ engineers to pinpoint the root cause of performance issues in Firefox Desktop and Androids

name: iQua Research Group — University of Toronto
description: Devised a max-min fairness algorithm in Python using SciPy and NetworkX, yielding up to a 300% increase in minimum flow bandwidth over baseline flows and significantly enhancing resource allocation across the network

Built a Node.js server to collect metrics (address, port, bandwidths) and a psycopg2-based CLI dashboard to rank 1,000+ connections from PostgreSQL, enabling coordinated communication across cloud nodes and benefiting research efforts
Extended 10+ unit tests in Rust to analyze TCP connection and transmission via evaluating packets

name: UofTHacks Organizer Team
description: Created reusable Next.js components and backend endpoints in Node.js for the website and applicant dashboard for the Canada’s first student-run hackathon, UofTHacks, benefiting over 1000+ applicants
